Content_Sketch: The camera turns off. You have seventeen seconds. That's when you make the crime.
Illustration_Note: Dark alley at night. Body camera red light switches OFF. Cop stickman stands over civilian. 17-second countdown visual.

Content_Sketch: You tap the bag through their pocket. You reach in. You pull it out.
Illustration_Note: Close-up: cop hand reaching into civilian pocket. Small plastic bag appears. Civilian stickman pinned against wall.

Content_Sketch: You don't find evidence. You place it.
Illustration_Note: Signature line. Bold text over dark background. Cop silhouette with badge visible.

Content_Sketch: You graduate the academy. You believe the badge means something.
Illustration_Note: Academy graduation. Cop stickman in fresh uniform receiving badge. Clean uniform - camera light ON.

Content_Sketch: First week on patrol. You watch a guilty man walk. Not enough evidence. The system lets him go.
Illustration_Note: Courtroom. Suspect walking free. Cop stickman watching from gallery - frustrated expression.

Content_Sketch: Your partner says: this isn't how it works.
Illustration_Note: Patrol car interior. Partner stickman (FACELESS) speaking. Cop stickman listening - expression shifting.

Content_Sketch: First time you watch your partner help the evidence. You say nothing. Your silence is the first yes.
Illustration_Note: Dark alley. Partner reaching into suspect pocket. Cop stickman standing behind - watching - saying nothing.

Content_Sketch: Your first plant. The suspect IS guilty - you know it. The evidence just isn't there. So you help.
Illustration_Note: Night street. Cop stickman planting small bag in suspect pocket. Suspect (FACELESS) face-down on hood of car.

Content_Sketch: The first time you do it - nothing happens. No questions. No report flagged. That's how you know it works.
Illustration_Note: Precinct desk. Cop stickman filing paperwork. Nobody looking. Nobody asking. Quiet office.

Content_Sketch: Commendation letter in your file. Sergeant shakes your hand. The system doesn't just stay silent. It rewards you.
Illustration_Note: Sergeant office. Sergeant stickman (FACELESS wider build) handing letter. Cop stickman receiving - posture straighter.

Content_Sketch: Second time is easier. Third time you don't think. You write it down. Ink makes it real.
Illustration_Note: Precinct desk close-up. Pen on paper. Report being written. Ink on white paper - the crime becoming official.

Content_Sketch: Body camera off is reflex now. Seventeen seconds. Quota rises. Sergeant asks why numbers are low.
Illustration_Note: Patrol car. Camera red light OFF. Whiteboard in precinct showing arrest numbers. Cop stickman checking camera.

Content_Sketch: The loop: You choose them. You prepare the story. You make it real. You swear to it.
Illustration_Note: 4-panel split: (1) pointing at target (2) writing report (3) planting bag (4) courtroom oath hand raised.

Content_Sketch: You don't pick criminals. You pick people no one will defend.
Illustration_Note: Target neighborhood. Cop stickman scanning street. Targeting civilian stickman (FACELESS) in hoodie - alone - no phone - no one waiting.

Content_Sketch: Your partner saw it. That's why they'll never say it. The public defender doesn't fight it. The system is too tired to check.
Illustration_Note: Split: (1) Partner looking away in car (2) Public defender stickman (FACELESS wrinkled suit) not reading file (3) Judge nodding.

Content_Sketch: Your name on a promotion list. Every arrest on that list is manufactured. You teach the rookie: this is how it works.
Illustration_Note: Precinct. Promotion board. Cop stickman showing rookie stickman (FACELESS shorter thinner) the method. Passing the system forward.

Content_Sketch: You stop checking first. Tonight it's different. You know they're innocent. Not maybe. You know.
Illustration_Note: Dark alley. Close-up cop face: cold flat expression. Civilian stickman (FACELESS) hands up - clearly no threat. The snap moment.

Content_Sketch: You don't find evidence. You place it. Same words. Different weight.
Illustration_Note: Signature line #2. Cop hand placing bag. Slow motion. Weight of repetition. Camera still OFF.

Content_Sketch: Internal Affairs calls. Your partner calls first. You drive through the neighborhood. Someone you arrested stands there. They don't run. They just look at you.
Illustration_Note: Split: (1) Phone ringing - IA (2) Partner on phone scared (3) Street - arrested person staring at patrol car. Not running. Just looking.

Content_Sketch: Not caught. Not punished. The system protects itself. You are the training officer now.
Illustration_Note: Precinct. Cop stickman (tallest now - grey temples - training insignia) standing over new rookies. Same posture partner had years ago.

Content_Sketch: Rookies learn the method. The method is the department. The department is the method.
Illustration_Note: Training room. Multiple rookie stickmen (FACELESS) taking notes. Cop stickman at front. The system replicating.

Content_Sketch: You try to remember one real arrest. You can't.
Illustration_Note: Cop stickman alone at desk. Arrest records spread out. Staring at files. Empty expression. Cannot distinguish real from manufactured.

Content_Sketch: The camera turns off. Seventeen seconds. That's all it ever took. You don't find evidence. You place it.
Illustration_Note: Mirror of HOOK. Same alley. Same camera off. Same posture. But now SUBJECT is tallest - grey - empty eyes. Signature line #3.

Content_Sketch: You can't tell which ones were real. You're not sure there were any.
Illustration_Note: Final text over black. Arrest record papers scattered. Fade to black.
